title: alnus rugosa var. serrulata
intro: Alnus rugosa var. serrulata, also known as the Mountain Alder or Red Alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the birch family Betulaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like alnusin, betulin, and alderone. The Mountain Alder is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function, and can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes.
